fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Utah Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Utah - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity utah, how to transfer audiologist license to utah, audiologist compact utah, utah audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Utah Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Utah Department of Commerce, Division of Professional Licensing (DOPL)
board.mailing_address: P.O. Box 146741, Salt Lake City, UT 84114-6741
board.physical_address: 160 E. 300 South, Salt Lake City, UT 84111

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Utah.

steps.title: Complete Education Requirements
steps.description: Complete a CAA or ACAE-accredited Doctor of Audiology (AuD) program and any required clinical practicum or fieldwork.

steps.title: Pass the Praxis
steps.description: Successfully pass the required Praxis Exam in Audiology.

steps.title: Submit Application and Fees
steps.description: Complete the state application, undergo a background check if required, and submit the necessary licensing fees.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Utah audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Utah have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Utah participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Utah without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Utah?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Utah while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.
